description: Test cases for iOS async wait and dynamic-content stabilization: ui_wait (5 modes idle/targetExists/targetGone/textExists/snapshotChanged — textGone does NOT exist), ui_waitAny multi-condition first-match-wins (conditions array 1-16, order is priority), wait_and_inspect combined helper, ui_inspect polling fallback for complex/cross-command conditions, timeout-as-business-result (matched:false), and static structure checks (formerly ios-dynamic-content)

evals.prompt: In an iOS app, after tapping a Refresh button a 'loading.spinner' element (accessibility identifier) appears. Wait for that spinner to disappear, with a 10-second timeout, then confirm the new content has rendered.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Trigger the refresh action (via ios-ui-form / ios-ui-list — NOT this skill's job)
2. Call ui_wait with mode='targetGone', accessibilityIdentifier='loading.spinner', timeoutMs=10000 (mode enum is idle/targetExists/targetGone/textExists/snapshotChanged — there is NO textGone mode; waiting for text to disappear must use targetGone on the view carrying that text)
3. intervalMs defaults to 100ms (range 50-5000); do NOT set 50ms — high-frequency ui_inspect polling on the main thread starves the very rendering being waited for
4. Response code 'ok' with data.matched=true on hit, or matched=false on timeout — TIMEOUT IS A BUSINESS RESULT, not a bug. Caller MUST read matched; matched=false + elapsedMs≈timeoutMs means timeout
5. After spinner gone, content is NOT necessarily rendered yet — call ui_wait(mode='idle', stableMs=300) OR wait_and_inspect to grab the fresh snapshot in one step
6. Old viewSnapshotID is now stale (view tree changed); subsequent operations must use a fresh ui_inspect

evals.prompt: After submitting a login form in an iOS app, wait for EITHER a 'Welcome back' text OR an 'Invalid credentials' text OR a 15-second timeout — whichever happens first. Report which one fired.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Submit the form (via ios-ui-form — NOT this skill's job)
2. Call ui_waitAny with conditions=[{id:'welcome', mode:'textExists', text:'Welcome back'}, {id:'invalid', mode:'textExists', text:'Invalid credentials'}, {id:'timeout_marker', mode:'idle'}], timeoutMs=15000, intervalMs=200
3. conditions array order IS priority — when two fire in the same poll, the earlier id wins (so put real outcomes before any idle/timeout sentinel)
4. Each condition needs its mode's required fields: textExists needs 'text'; targetExists/targetGone need accessibilityIdentifier or path; snapshotChanged needs viewSnapshotID; idle needs nothing extra
5. timeoutMs/intervalMs/stableMs/includeHidden are SHARED across all conditions (top-level, not per-condition)
6. Response: data.matched (bool), data.matchedID (the winning condition's id), data.matchedIndex, data.elapsedMs
7. On 15s timeout: matched=false, matchedID=null — caller treats this as 'neither Welcome nor Invalid appeared', which is itself a meaningful test result (network hang / unknown state)

evals.prompt: After pushing a new screen in an iOS app, wait for the push transition animation to finish before reading the new screen's content. The new screen has no known accessibility identifier ahead of time.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Before triggering the push, call ui_inspect to capture the current viewSnapshotID (this is the BEFORE snapshot)
2. Trigger the push (via ios-ui-nav — NOT this skill's job)
3. Call ui_wait(mode='snapshotChanged', viewSnapshotID='<the-before-snapshot-id>', timeoutMs=3000) — snapshotChanged compares the current view tree to the referenced snapshot and fires on ANY change, which is exactly what a push transition produces
4. snapshotChanged REQUIRES viewSnapshotID (from a prior ui_inspect) — without it, returns invalid_data. Watch the 120s TTL: if the before-snapshot was taken too early it may expire
5. Alternatively use mode='idle' with stableMs=300-500 for a fixed settle window (push transitions take 200-500ms) when capturing a pre-snapshot is awkward
6. After the wait returns, call ui_inspect (or wait_and_inspect to combine steps) — the old viewSnapshotID is now stale; navigationBar.title / targets must come from the fresh inspect
7. Do NOT call ui_wait with mode='textGone' — that mode does not exist in the schema

evals.prompt: In an iOS app, a complex post-submit state requires checking three things in sequence: first an alert appears, then after dismissing it a loading indicator shows, then after loading clears a results table appears. Implement this multi-step wait.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Recognize this is a CROSS-COMMAND sequential condition — ui_waitAny cannot express 'A then B then C'; it waits for the first of parallel conditions, not a sequence. Use the ui_inspect POLLING FALLBACK pattern (chain multiple ui_wait calls or poll ui_inspect manually)
2. Step 1: wait for alert — ui_wait(mode='textExists', text='<alert-title>') OR poll ui_inspect checking alert.available==true. Do NOT use ui_wait to RESPOND to the alert — that goes through ui_alert_respond (ios-ui-alert)
3. Dismiss the alert via ui_alert_respond (ios-ui-alert — NOT this skill)
4. Step 2: ui_wait(mode='targetGone', accessibilityIdentifier='loading.spinner', timeoutMs=10000) for the loading phase
5. Step 3: ui_wait(mode='targetExists', accessibilityIdentifier='results.table', timeoutMs=10000) OR wait_and_inspect to land directly on the fresh snapshot with the table visible
6. Each step reads data.matched before proceeding; on matched=false the sequence aborts with a specific phase failure (alert-never-came / loading-never-cleared / table-never-appeared) rather than running blindly into the next step
7. Network-roundtrip rationale: three ui_wait calls (each server-side polling) is far cheaper than a bash loop doing ui_inspect on every iteration — prefer ui_wait / ui_waitAny / wait_and_inspect, drop to ui_inspect polling only when conditions are genuinely cross-command or need business computation

evals.prompt: An iOS test author wants a single call that waits for a 'Saved' toast to appear after tapping Save, and immediately returns the refreshed UI snapshot for follow-up assertions. Wait up to 5 seconds.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Use wait_and_inspect — the combined helper that runs ui_waitAny THEN ui_inspect in one call, returning both the wait verdict and the fresh targets/viewSnapshotID
2. conditions=[{id:'saved', mode:'textExists', text:'Saved'}], timeoutMs=5000
3. inspectOptions may carry ui_inspect fields (maxDepth, accessibilityIdentifier, textLimit, maxTargets) but MUST NOT carry detailLevel (that's ui_topViewHierarchy-only) or conditions (that's ui_waitAny-only) — inspectOptions accepts only real ui_inspect fields
4. On hit: response contains the ui_waitAny result (matched=true, matchedID='saved', elapsedMs) PLUS a fresh ui_inspect result (targets[], alert, navigationBar, viewSnapshotID)
5. On timeout: wait_and_inspect STILL returns the latest observation — matched=false but the inspect result is present, so the caller can see what state the app was actually in at timeout (no lost data)
6. Subsequent ui_tap / ui_control_sendAction calls must use the viewSnapshotID from this inspect result, not any earlier one — the Save tap changed the view tree
7. This saves one round-trip vs calling ui_wait then ui_inspect separately

evals.prompt: Check the static structure of the ios-ui-wait SKILL.md (offline, no app run): frontmatter must have name / description / allowed-tools; every allowed-tools entry must be a real iOSDriver MCP tool that the body actually calls; the body must NOT list a textGone mode (it does not exist); the body must not hardcode SPMExample values; the body must NOT carry the old 'ui.wait commands not fully tested yet' caveat.
evals.expected_output:
Should verify offline (no app run required):
1. Frontmatter has all three fields present — grep -E '^(name|description|allowed-tools):' returns exactly 3
2. allowed-tools lists exactly these 4 real iOSDriver tools: mcp__iOSDriver__ui_wait, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_waitAny, mcp__iOSDriver__wait_and_inspect, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_inspect (ui_inspect is the polling fallback; no ui_tap / ui_input / ui_screenshot listed because actions are delegated to other skills)
3. name field value matches directory name (ios-ui-wait)
4. description contains the transition keyword '原 ios-dynamic-content' for old-prompt routing (old skill name was ios-dynamic-content)
5. Body references the real wait vocabulary — grep -ic 'ui_wait\|ui_waitAny\|wait_and_inspect' returns >= 1
6. Body enumerates the CORRECT five ui_wait modes: idle / targetExists / targetGone / textExists / snapshotChanged — grep for 'textGone' as a mode name should return 0 matches in the capabilities/mode table (textGone may appear only in the 'does not exist' correction section)
7. Body does NOT carry the old caveat 'not fully tested yet' / 'not fully tested' — grep -ic 'not fully tested' returns 0 (the actions ARE registered and tested per current help/schema)
8. No SPMExample hardcoding — grep -nE 'com\.coo\.SPMExample|065CC8DB|00008030' returns empty (OK)
9. Body covers preserved capabilities: element appear/disappear (targetExists/targetGone), multi-condition wait (ui_waitAny conditions), timeout handling (matched:false as business result), loading indicator handling, snapshotChanged + viewSnapshotID, idle stable window, ui_inspect polling fallback, and the wait_and_inspect combined helper
10. Key corrections surfaced: textGone does not exist (use targetGone on the view carrying the text); timeout returns code:ok + matched:false (not an error); loading-gone ≠ content-rendered (needs follow-up idle wait or wait_and_inspect)
